rows.notes: Clear, large heading at top left of page; no monetary columns after it. Vertical ruling lines for amounts not used on this row.

The phrase 'Brought over' is legible. The adjacent numbers at the top-right read as '4 10 8' though there is additional small script nearby and the alignment is ambiguous; chosen 4 pounds 10 shillings 8 pence as the best single reading.
Description is clear. Amount is two small figures in the shillings/pence columns read as '5 5' (5s 5d). Alignment with the vertical ruling is clear.
Short entry 'Bessington Do' (Do = ditto). Amount clearly reads '2 2' in the shillings and pence columns (2s 2d).
Entry 'Gwinear Do' legible. Amount reads like '5 2' (5s 2d). The tens-digit of the pence is slightly faint but the 2 is the most consistent reading with the ruling.
Description partially abbreviated as 'Diocesan Bd of Education'. To the right is a clear '10' in the shillings column and no pence; interpreted as 10s 0d (no pounds written).

Handwriting of the description is somewhat compressed ('Jacob Living ...'); the amount to the right is a single '5' in the shilling column with no pence; read as 5s 0d. Slight uncertainty in the precise name but amount alignment is acceptable.
Description reads 'Widows & Orphans of Navy Do' (abbreviated). Two figures '5 5' are visible in the shillings and pence columns; ink is a bit faded but alignment supports 5s 5d.
Clear phrase 'University Marshal'. A single '2' in the shillings column is legible; no pence noted. Read as 2s 0d.
The entry 'Bell ringer & Chapel Clerk' is readable. To the right is a clear '12' in the shillings column and no pence; interpreted as 12s 0d.
Label 'Corn-prices' is legible. The right-hand numbers read '19 6' aligned under shillings and pence (19s 6d). The shillings value is at the high end (19) but within valid range.
Clearly reads 'Easter Offerings'. Small figures '1 1' visible in shillings and pence columns (1s 1d).
Sub-item under 'College Gardeners' clearly marked '1st Do to Xmas 1843'. Amount reads as three columns '1 9 4' interpreted as £1 9s 4d. Vertical alignment matches ruling.

Second line '2 do to 1 day 1844' — the three-digit grouping is ambiguous; read as £9 16s 0d because the left-most column has a '9' and the shillings column '16'. Slight uncertainty because the tens-position is somewhat faint.
Readable as '3 do to ... 1844'. Amount appears as '12 0 9' which aligns to £12 0s 9d; pence '9' is clear.
Line '4 do to Michs 1844' and numbers '7 15 8' are clear and well aligned. Interpreted as £7 15s 8d with good legibility.
Clear centered section heading 'Open Scholarships' with no amounts on this line.
Name 'Mr Cosburne' (handwriting consistent). Right-hand columns show '40' in the pounds column and blanks for shillings/pence -> £40 0s 0d. Very clear.
Clear name 'Mr Godley'. Pounds column shows '40' aligned with previous scholarship entries; read as £40 0s 0d.
Entry 'Mr Whitehead' with same clear '40' in pounds column. Interpreted as £40 0s 0d.
Label 'Synodals & Procuration' readable. To the right a solitary '12' aligns under shillings column; read as 12s 0d. The exact placement is slightly offset so confidence is moderate.

Section label 'Soluta Varia' appears adjacent to a well-aligned larger figure '616 9 3' in the amount columns; treated as a subtotal/total for the preceding section. Numbers are clear though the heading overlaps slightly with adjacent script.

rows.description: Mr Welsh's bill for the yr ending Nov 2 1843
rows.notes: Description is long but legible. The amount reads '64 6 6' aligned in the pounds/shillings/pence columns; all digits are readable though small.
Single-word entry 'Stinger' (could be another spelling); amount reads '12 9' in shillings/pence columns. The word is slightly ambiguous and might be 'Stinger' or 'Stringer' so confidence is moderate.

rows.description: Capel Mason's work on Campfield Farm
rows.notes: Description 'Capel Mason's work on Campfield Farm' readable with an amount '2 15' clearly in the pounds/shillings columns; interpreted as £2 15s 0d.

rows.description: Gresham Rector's prize for Mr G---
rows.notes: Line appears to read 'Gresham - Rector's prize for Mr G---'. Amount '9 18 6' is clearly written as £9 18s 6d. The recipient name is partially abbreviated; amount is clear.

This set shows a grouped expense with sub-lines; the summed line reads '8 3 9 1/2' — interpreted as £8 3s 9½d (halfpenny recorded as 0.5). The fraction symbol is present but slightly stylised; chosen 0.5 (halfpenny).
Description legible as 'Repairs of Bushey Tenements'. The amount at right looks like a '5' but the row is slightly crowded by adjacent sub-items; recorded as 5s 0d with moderate confidence.
Text 'Falkner allowance for planting acorns' readably transcribed. Amount looks like a single '1' in the pounds column; interpreted as £1 0s 0d. Slight uncertainty due to small script.

Line begins 'Dale valuing Ridlington Rectory Farm ...' Handwriting of description slightly cramped. Amount appears as '5 5' and read as £5 5s 0d. The name 'Ridlington' is somewhat uncertain (reading of initial letters).

rows.description: Mr Wollcombe money advanced by him
rows.notes: Description 'Mr Wollcombe money advanced by him' is abbreviated and the reading of the surname is slightly uncertain. Amount reads '19 7' in pounds/shillings according to alignment; interpreted as £19 7s 0d.

rows.description: The redemption of the Coll plate signed by (illegible)
rows.notes: Sentence-like entry referring to 'redemption of the Coll plate' with a signature note. Amount to the right reads '12 10' - read as £12 10s 0d. The final parenthetical name is illegible hence moderate confidence.
Line 'Grass for Venison June 30. 1843' is readable. Amount reads '6 8' which was taken as £6 8s 0d; some slight smudge near digits reduces confidence.

rows.description: Mrs Rose kitchen woman allowance at 5/- per wk from March 6 1843 to (March) 1843 42 weeks
rows.notes: Long descriptive line about Mrs Rose's allowance; the amount at right reads '10 10' which aligns as £10 10s 0d. The duration text is cramped but the numeric amount is reasonably clear.

Bottom boxed total shows numerals '145 16 0 1/2' in the amount columns; interpreted as £145 16s 0½d (halfpenny recorded as 0.5). The boxed style and alignment indicate this is the page total. Description line above is the last textual entry, so the boxed numbers appear without descriptive text.
